other_observers: Paul Neldner saw it at 3:00 this afternoon; Polly Wren Neldner
duration: Total length of observation approximately 45 minutes.
specific_location: Approx. 3/4 mile east of La Veta at Wahatoya State Wildlife area.
description: Very large tern, larger that Ring-billed Gull in vicinity. More than twice as large as Black Tern and Forster's Tern, both of which were flying in the vicinity. All black cap, stout dark red bill with slightly dusky tip. Black legs. In flight wingbeats were shallow, with dark under the primaries.Behaviors: Bird was at first resting on a small island in the center of the lake. Later it flew to the opposite shore. Would fly quite high up, with short, stiff wing beats.Call: Harsh, deep almost screamPlumage: Adult breeding
photographer: Photographer: Polly Wren Neldner, Polly Wren Neldner, Polly Wren Neldner, Polly Wren Neldner
